discovery.run_summary: Risk-off in tech/semis with SMH down nearly 5% and QQQ off ~2%, while financials, industrials, and the Dow are green — classic rotation day. Semiconductor equipment names that ripped over the past 5 days (VECO +12%, ONTO +15%, AEIS +12%) are giving back hard with no fresh company-specific catalysts, suggesting positioning unwind rather than fundamental news.

notable_index_moves: SMH -4.8% and XLK -2.8% drive QQQ -1.9% while DIA +0.58% and XLF +1.47% — sharp intraday rotation out of semis/AI into financials and cyclicals
VIX only 16.4 (+1.3%) — selling is sector-specific, not broad risk-off
AVGO -4.4%, TSM -3.5%, NVDA -2.4% leading the semi weakness

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-equipment small-cap giving back a sharp 5-day rally with no company-specific catalyst on a sector-wide SMH down day
discoveries.thesis: VECO ran up notably over the past week alongside the semi-equipment cohort (ONTO, AEIS up similar amounts) and is now unwinding harder than the SMH index because of low float and momentum positioning. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this is sector beta plus crowding unwind, and an outsized one-day drop in a name that just rallied tends to overshoot fundamentals.
discoveries.what_confirms: If SMH stabilizes tomorrow and VECO continues to trade weak independently, or if a peer/customer (LRCX, AMAT) pre-announces caution, the move is justified.
discoveries.what_kills: If a delayed news source surfaces a VECO-specific negative (customer push-out, downgrade with channel checks), the move is fundamentally driven and not a fade.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When three peers in the same narrow industry (VECO/ONTO/AEIS) all rallied ~12-15% in 5 days and all give back sharply together on a sector-down day with empty catalyst signals, it's almost always crowded-trade unwind rather than news — fade candidates if the sector base holds.
discoveries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; sympathy with SMH -4.8% and broader semi sell-off (AVGO, TSM, NVDA all down hard)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty {}, no 8-K, no earnings; the 5-day +12% paired with sector-wide semi weakness today is the entire story.
research_pointers: Check if VECO is in any leveraged semi ETF that rebalanced today
Scan for any sell-side downgrade or channel-check note timestamped today
Compare VECO's drawdown to ONTO/AEIS — if it's the worst of the three with no fundamental reason, that's the fade signal
Look at short interest and recent options flow for crowding evidence

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap-equipment-adjacent name unwinding a 5-day rally on a sector-down day; recent 8-K is a low-content 8.01 filing
discoveries.thesis: Same pattern as VECO/ONTO — AEIS rallied substantially over 5 days, today gives back on broad semi weakness. The June 12 8-K is items 8.01/9.01 only (catch-all + exhibits), which is almost never severely material on its own. Below-average volume on the down move suggests profit-taking, not panic.
discoveries.what_confirms: If the 8-K exhibit (worth reading) is a benign investor-day deck or boilerplate, and SMH bounces while AEIS lags less, fade looks right.
discoveries.what_kills: If the 8.01 filing contained a soft pre-announcement or guidance reset language, the move is justified and not a fade.
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with semi-cap weakness; possible minor digestion of June 12 8-K
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K from 4 days ago is items 8.01 + 9.01 only, with below-average volume today (0.67x) — pattern argues against fresh material bad news.
research_pointers: Read the June 12 8-K exhibit — what was disclosed?
Compare AEIS drawdown to ONTO/VECO to gauge cohort consistency
Check whether AEIS is in any newly-rebalanced industrial/semi index

discoveries.name: PTC Therapeutics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Biotech down sharply on 4x volume with a same-day 8-K item 8.01 — the filing exhibit is the key unknown
discoveries.thesis: Volume 4x average paired with a same-day 8.01 + 9.01 filing strongly suggests company-specific news drove the move, but 8.01 is a catch-all and without reading the exhibit, the direction can't be inferred reliably. In biotech, an 8.01 same-day with a 4%+ drop often means a clinical-trial readout, regulatory update, or licensing news — could be a partial setback (overdone candidate) or a genuine pipeline hit (justified). XBI is only -0.78%, so this is idiosyncratic.
discoveries.what_confirms: Reading the 8-K exhibit will resolve direction immediately.
discoveries.what_kills: Without the exhibit content, no thesis can be defended at conf 3+.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Same-day 8.01 + 4x volume in biotech is always exhibit-driven — never classify before reading the exhibit; the code alone is non-diagnostic.
discoveries.catalyst: Same-day 8-K (items 8.01/9.01) — exhibit content unknown without reading
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 4x average volume on day of 8-K filing — confirms the filing is the catalyst, but item 8.01 doesn't reveal direction.
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibit immediately — likely a press release on clinical data, regulatory communication, or partnership
Check PTCT pipeline calendar (Translarna, Upstaza, sepiapterin) for any expected catalyst dates
Cross-check biotech twitter / FierceBiotech for the news angle

discoveries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Apple-supplier semi name down with the SMH selloff, but with a June 12 item 1.01 (material agreement) 8-K that could cut either way
discoveries.thesis: QRVO is moving roughly in line with the broader semi/Apple-supply complex on a clear sector-down day, but the June 12 1.01 filing (material definitive agreement) is non-trivial — could be M&A, a big customer contract, a debt covenant amendment, or a divestiture. Without reading the exhibit, the direction the 1.01 should push the stock is unknowable, and today's move is consistent with pure semi-tape sympathy regardless.
discoveries.what_confirms: If the 1.01 is a benign refinancing or standard contract, today's move is pure sympathy and likely fadeable; if it's a strategic divestiture or major customer loss, the move is fundamental.
discoveries.what_kills: Without the exhibit, neither side rises above conf 3.
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with semi/Apple-supplier weakness; possible digestion of June 12 material agreement
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Move size is in line with semi peers (AVGO -4.4%, TSM -3.5%); 8-K item 1.01 from 4 days ago is the only company-specific signal but direction unknown without exhibit.
research_pointers: Read the June 12 8-K exhibit to determine what the material agreement is
Check for any Apple supply-chain commentary tied to the Intel 18A-P production news today (potential modem/RF reshuffle implications)
Compare QRVO drawdown to SWKS for relative cohort behavior

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Broad semiconductor sell-off (SMH -4.8%, AVGO/TSM/NVDA all sharply down) on no obvious single catalyst — possibly rotation into financials post-Fed/Warsh news and AI compute valuation digestion
Intel begins production of 18A-P chip node, inching closer to Apple deal

ai_announcements.headline: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO
ai_announcements.summary: SpaceX acquiring AI coding tool Cursor at $60B, citing $26T AI TAM. Major consolidation move in AI coding space.

ai_announcements.headline: US government's Anthropic models ban
ai_announcements.summary: Trump administration forced Anthropic to pull latest cybersecurity models. Signals AI industry exposure to government interference. Anthropic-related call - flagging for separate bias grading.

ai_announcements.headline: ChatGPT market share slips below 50%, Claude at 245M MAU
ai_announcements.summary: Competitive landscape shift: OpenAI losing share to Gemini (662M) and Claude (245M). Anthropic-adjacent data point.

ai_announcements.headline: Android 17 launches with expanded Gemini features
ai_announcements.summary: Google embeds latest AI models deeper into Android/Pixel devices.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: The stocks that moved today (PTCT, VECO, ONTO, AEIS, QRVO) do not have clear linkage to today's AI announcements. VECO/ONTO/AEIS are semicap equipment names that ran up 12-15% over the prior 5 days and are giving back gains - this looks like profit-taking/sector rotation in AI-infrastructure beneficiaries, NOT a reaction to any specific AI headline today. No SpaceX/Cursor, Anthropic, or Google news in the feed directly threatens these names. PTCT is biotech with an 8-K filing - unrelated to AI. QRVO had a material definitive agreement 8-K on 6/12 - likely the driver, not AI news. Without a named mechanism connecting today's AI announcements to these specific tickers, calling a verdict would be manufacturing signal. Worth monitoring VECO/ONTO/AEIS for a continued semicap pullback thesis (potentially OVERDONE given strong 5-day base), but that's a sector-technical call, not an AI-news call.
